candidates.bio: Republican U.S. Representative for Alaska's at-large district, first elected in 2024 after defeating Mary Peltola. Businessman and member of the prominent Begich political family.

MILLER-MEEKS, Mariannette, a Representative from Iowa; born in Herlong, Lassen County, Calif., September 6, 1955; attended San Antonio Junior College, San Antonio, Texas; B.S.N., Texas Christian University, Fort Worth, Texas, 1976; M.S., University of Southern California, Los Angeles, Calif., 1980; M.D., University of Texas Health Science Center, San Antonio, Texas, 1986; United States Army; United States Army Reserve; assistant professor, University of Michigan, 1991-1994; assistant professor, University of Iowa, 1994 – 1997; ophthalmologist; director, Iowa Department of Public Health, 2010 – 2013; unsuccessful candidate for election to the One Hundred Eleventh Congress in 2008, One Hundred Twelfth Congress in 2010, and One Hundred Fourteenth Congress in 2014; member of the Iowa state senate, 2019 – 2020; elected as a Republican to the One Hundred Seventeenth Congress (January 3, 2021 – Present).

University of Iowa law professor and former state representative; presumptive Democratic nominee (frontrunner ahead of the June 2, 2026 primary). Making her third run after losing to Miller-Meeks by under 800 votes in 2024.
Republican U.S. Representative for Iowa's 3rd district, first elected in 2022. Former state senator and U.S. Air Force veteran.
Democratic Iowa state senator and presumptive Democratic nominee (frontrunner ahead of the June 2026 primary after rivals dropped out and endorsed her).
Democratic U.S. Representative for Indiana's 1st district (Northwest Indiana), first elected in 2020. Former Calumet Township trustee.
Republican nominee; Lake County councilor and Lake County GOP chair who won the May 2026 primary. Was also the 2024 GOP nominee against Mrvan.

Democratic U.S. Representative for North Carolina's 1st district, first elected in 2022. Former state senator and U.S. Air Force veteran; among the most vulnerable House Democrats after redistricting.
Republican nominee; retired Army colonel who narrowly lost to Davis in 2024, won the March 2026 GOP primary, setting up a rematch in the redrawn, more Republican-leaning district.
Republican U.S. Representative for North Carolina's 11th district (western NC), first elected in 2022. Former state senator and business owner.
Democratic nominee; Fairview farmer and small-business owner who won the March 2026 primary with about 65% and was added to the DCCC 'Red to Blue' program.
Democratic nominee for the open Omaha-area 'blue dot' 2nd district; won a crowded May 2026 primary. The seat is open after Republican Rep. Don Bacon's retirement.
Republican nominee; Omaha city councilman who ran unopposed for the GOP nomination and is endorsed by President Trump.
Democratic U.S. Representative for Ohio's 1st district (Cincinnati area), first elected in 2022. Former Cincinnati city councilman and schoolteacher.
Republican nominee; won the May 2026 GOP primary with President Trump's endorsement to challenge Landsman in the gerrymandered, more Republican-leaning district.

KAPTUR, Marcia Carolyn (Marcy), a Representative from Ohio; born in Toledo, Lucas County, Ohio, June 17, 1946; graduated from St. Ursula Academy, Toledo, Ohio, 1964; B.A., University of Wisconsin, Madison, Wis., 1968; M.A., University of Michigan, Ann Arbor, Mich., 1974; attended the University of Manchester, Manchester, England, 1974; post-graduate studies, Massachusetts Institute of Technology, Cambridge, Mass., 1981; urban planner, Toledo-Lucas County, Ohio, Plan Commissions, 1969-1975; director of planning, National Center for Urban Ethnic Affairs, 1975-1977; assistant director for urban affairs, domestic policy staff, Executive Office of the President, 1977-1979; elected as a Democrat to the Ninety-eighth and to the seventeen succeeding Congresses (January 3, 1983-present).
Republican nominee; former state representative who won the May 2026 GOP primary, setting up a rematch after losing to Kaptur by roughly 2,400 votes in 2024.
Democratic U.S. Representative for Oregon's 5th district, first elected in 2024 after defeating Republican Lori Chavez-DeRemer. Former state representative.
Republican nominee; Deschutes County commissioner who won the May 2026 GOP primary with about 57%.
Republican U.S. Representative for Pennsylvania's 1st district (Bucks County), first elected in 2016. Former FBI agent and co-chair of the Problem Solvers Caucus.
Democratic nominee; vice chair of the Bucks County Board of Commissioners who won the May 2026 primary and was added to the DCCC 'Red to Blue' program.
Republican U.S. Representative for Pennsylvania's 7th district (Lehigh Valley), first elected in 2024 by defeating Democrat Susan Wild. Former state representative.
Democratic nominee; retired firefighter and firefighters-union president who won the May 2026 primary with endorsements from Bernie Sanders and Gov. Josh Shapiro.
Republican U.S. Representative for Pennsylvania's 8th district (Scranton/Wilkes-Barre), first elected in 2024. Businessman who flipped the seat from Democrat Matt Cartwright.
Democratic nominee; mayor of Scranton, unopposed in the May 2026 primary. Campaigning on Bresnahan's congressional stock-trading record.

PERRY, Scott, a Representative from Pennsylvania; born in San Diego, San Diego County, Calif., May 27, 1962; B.S., Pennsylvania State University, University Park, Pa., 1991; Pennsylvania Army National Guard, 1980-present; business owner; member of the Pennsylvania state house of representatives, 2007-2012; elected as a Republican to the One Hundred Thirteenth and to the two succeeding Congresses (January 3, 2013-present).
Democratic nominee; former WGAL news anchor who won the May 2026 primary by a wide margin, setting up a rematch after losing to Perry by about 1 point in 2024.
Republican U.S. Representative for Texas's 15th district (South Texas), first elected in 2022. First Republican to hold the seat.
Democratic nominee; Tejano music star who won the March 2026 Democratic primary with about 68%.
Republican nominee; gun-rights YouTuber ('The AK Guy') who advanced for the open seat after Rep. Tony Gonzales withdrew and later resigned in 2026.
Democratic nominee; won the March 2026 Democratic primary for the open West Texas 23rd district seat.

CUELLAR, Henry, a Representative from Texas; born in Laredo, Webb County, Tex., September 19, 1955; graduated from J.W. Nixon High School, Laredo, Tex., 1974; A.A., Laredo Community College, Laredo, Tex., 1976; B.S., Georgetown University, Washington, D.C., 1978; J.D., University of Texas, Austin, Tex., 1981; M.A., Texas A&M International University, Laredo, Tex., 1982; Ph.D., University of Texas, Austin, Tex., 1998; lawyer, private practice; member of the Texas state house of representatives, 1987-2001; unsuccessful candidate for nomination to the United States of Representatives in 2002; Texas secretary of state, 2001; elected as a Democrat to the One Hundred Ninth and to the six succeeding Congresses (January 3, 2005-present).
Republican nominee; Webb County Judge who won the March 2026 GOP primary to challenge Cuellar in the redrawn, more Republican district.

GONZALEZ, Vicente Jr., a Representative from Texas; born in Corpus Christi, Nueces County, Tex., September 4, 1967; earned G.E.D., 1985; B.A., Embry-Riddle Aeronautical University, Naval Air Station, Corpus Christi, Tex., 1992; J.D., Texas Wesleyan School of Law, Fort Worth, Tex., 1996; lawyer, private practice; elected as a Democrat to the One Hundred Fifteenth Congress (January 3, 2017-present).
Republican nominee; Army veteran who won the March 2026 GOP primary with Trump's endorsement, defeating former Rep. Mayra Flores in the redrawn South Texas district.
Democratic nominee; Bexar County sheriff's deputy who won the May 2026 Democratic runoff for the redrawn open 35th district seat (Rep. Greg Casar was drawn out).
Republican nominee; retired Air Force veteran who won the May 2026 GOP runoff with Trump's endorsement.
Republican U.S. Senator from Alaska, first elected in 2014. Former Alaska attorney general and state Department of Natural Resources commissioner; U.S. Marine Corps veteran.
Democratic nominee; former U.S. Representative for Alaska's at-large district (2022-2025), the first Alaska Native elected to Congress. Lost her 2024 House reelection and is now challenging Sullivan.

Democratic U.S. Senator from Georgia, first elected in the January 2021 runoff. Former documentary filmmaker and investigative journalist; one of the most vulnerable 2026 Democratic incumbents in a Trump-won state. Republican opponent to be decided in a June 16, 2026 runoff (Mike Collins vs. Derek Dooley).
Democratic nominee for the open North Carolina Senate seat; former two-term governor (2017-2025) and state attorney general. Won the March 2026 Democratic primary with about 92%.
Republican nominee; former Republican National Committee chairman and former North Carolina GOP chair, won the March 2026 GOP primary.

Republican U.S. Senator from Ohio, appointed in 2025 to fill JD Vance's seat after Vance became Vice President. Former Ohio lieutenant governor and secretary of state; running in the 2026 special election.
Democratic nominee; former three-term U.S. Senator (2007-2025) seeking a comeback after losing to Bernie Moreno in 2024. Won the 2026 Democratic primary for the special election.
Democratic nominee for the open Texas Senate seat; state representative who won the March 2026 Democratic primary and set first-quarter Senate fundraising records (over $27M in Q1 2026).
Republican nominee; Texas attorney general who defeated incumbent Sen. John Cornyn 64-36 in the May 2026 GOP runoff with Trump's endorsement.
